summary: Iteration 4 testing complete. All content / language simplification changes verified on both backend and frontend. 10/10 backend pytest tests pass (5 estimator + 4 SEO + 1 simplified-language). Frontend Playwright checks confirm: hero city-pills/star-rating/'Garansi Konstruksi 1 Tahun'/'Transparansi 100%' removed; bento card has the 3 new supporting points; no hero-stat-block; WhyUs has 6 cards with the exact expected titles in order; FAQ has exactly 5 items with the 'Saya di luar 4 kota' entry removed and simpler phrasing applied; AI estimator end-to-end on mobile (390x844) returns a result using simplified categories ('Pondasi & Struktur', 'Dinding & Atap', 'Listrik, Air & Sanitasi', 'Finishing & Interior', 'Tukang & Pengawasan') with no MEP/Mekanikal jargon and labels 'Perkiraan Total', 'Lama pengerjaan', 'Rincian Biaya' showing correctly; hero WA href contains wa.me/6282226817232; 5 services cards present; floating WhatsApp appears after scroll.
critical_code_review_comments: Estimator.jsx left-rail bullet still says 'Breakdown per kategori (struktur, MEP, finishing)' (line ~162). The user-facing copy was supposed to drop the 'MEP' jargon — this bullet should be updated to e.g. 'Rincian biaya per kategori (struktur, listrik/air, penyelesaian)' to match the iteration 4 simplification mandate. Non-blocking but inconsistent with the new wording.

Backend prompt in server.py instructs the LLM to use a category literally named 'Finishing & Interior' (line 158) but the same prompt (line 176) tells the LLM to AVOID the word 'finishing'. The LLM unsurprisingly keeps the literal category name, so the breakdown still surfaces the word 'Finishing'. If the goal is truly zero-jargon, rename the category in the prompt to 'Penyelesaian & Interior' or similar.

Hero.jsx still ends the bento risk-reversal pill with 'tanpa biaya, tanpa komitmen.' (line 262). The iteration 4 brief asked to replace 'tanpa komitmen' with 'tanpa keharusan lanjut' (done in CTABand.jsx and FAQSection.jsx) — consider applying the same wording here for consistency.

Hydration warning in dev build: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' originates from Emergent's dev-mode auto-injected debug spans on the <option> elements inside Estimator's selects. Not a real code bug, but visible in console; harmless for production build.
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest entrypoint: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v (10 tests total, ~50s due to live LLM calls). Existing test files: test_estimator_seo.py (iteration 3 — estimator basic + SEO), test_iteration4_simple_lang.py (iteration 4 — jargon-free categories + simple summary). Estimator hits live Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-5 via Emergent LLM key, expect 15-30s per call. Note 'Finishing & Interior' is an accepted category name even though 'finishing' is technically jargon — backend prompt explicitly uses it. WhyUs eyebrow text in source is '— Kenapa Pilih Timur Design' but renders uppercase via tracking-luxe class; case-sensitive substring tests must account for this.
